rationale: Security must stay explicit and not disappear inside a merged umbrella
Multi-tenant SaaS should activate tenant isolation
Separate modularity from modifiability in a future-plugin scenario
Explicit admin audit-trail requirements should activate compliance_auditability even without a formal regulated regime
ADR must explain why the winner won without weighted-score theater
Confidence must reflect evidence quality rather than overclaim measurement
Small generalist team should trigger cognitive load
ADR should summarize overall strengths and weaknesses instead of hiding everything in row detail
False-positive bait: no disaster recovery requirement exists in the SaaS brief
Chosen architecture should acknowledge a meaningful weakness rather than score everything as strong
Explicit dashboard SLO should activate performance
Four external systems should activate integration complexity
Different release cadences should activate deployment independence
Explicitly low operational staffing plus async complexity should force mitigation details when operability weakens
False-positive bait: internal enterprise platform is not multi-tenant
Generic architecture slogans should fail the rationale bar
Scorecard must define a review trigger tied to system change
Activated optional axes need a cited activation source, not silent inclusion
Planted vague assumptions should be sharpened into falsifiable terms rather than copied through
Low-headcount operations cost should appear as an explicit tradeoff, not vanish behind architecture slogans
Replayability and observability do not automatically create compliance_auditability without a real evidentiary or regulatory trigger
Regulated payments should activate compliance and auditability
Ledger invariants and async side effects should activate data consistency
A candidate with catastrophic security or consistency cannot win on cost alone
Weak-axis mitigation needs owner, enforcement, and deadline
Runner-up comparison and delta should make the tradeoff auditable
Regression seed to preserve separate modularity scoring after the scorecard change
False-positive bait: regulated coordinated release windows should not auto-activate deployment independence
Critical-axis blocking should follow dominant quality attributes and hard requirements, not a generic fixed list
Re-evaluation triggers should include concrete structural triggers for async growth and topology change
Runner-up comparison should use a plausible alternative rather than a straw man
When candidate fit is close, Adaptability First should break the tie explicitly rather than letting raw match pretend to decide it
A thin-evidence concept should trigger research escalation or provisional scoring when too many core axes would otherwise be assumed
Latency-sensitive product shape alone should not activate performance without an explicit latency or throughput target
Every ADR must state where __specs__ and __tests__ live for the selected pattern
